provider.name: Coastal Elements AI (with Poly186 delivery team)

contacts.name: Dylan Ryan
contacts.notes: Primary business and account lead; local to Hampton Roads; responsible for setting up follow-ups and workshops.

contacts.name: Princeps Polycap
contacts.role: Technical Lead (poly186.io, delivery partner to Coastal Elements AI)
contacts.notes: Leads AI/LLM architecture, platform choices, and implementation details.

contacts.name: Drew (last name not stated)
contacts.notes: Coordinates teams, timelines, resources, and client communication for deliveries.

contacts.name: Richard
contacts.notes: Made the initial connection between Dylan/Coastal Elements AI and S.L. Nusbaum; works with brokerage group mentioned by Mark.

client.name: S.L. Nusbaum Realty Co. (referred to as S.L. Nesbaum / Nesbom)

contacts.name: Mark Ashley
contacts.notes: Primary client contact; oversees IT, Azure environment, 365/Copilot, and AI governance for corporate plus multifamily portfolio.

deliverables: Initial AI/automation discovery discussions with IT leadership (completed in this call).
Proposed workshop framework for departmental discovery (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily operations, Brokerage, etc.).
Questionnaire/document with discovery questions to use in departmental meetings to surface AI/automation use cases and KPIs/OKRs.
High-level assessment of existing AI-related tools (e.g., current utility-bill bot, AI features in main line-of-business app, Microsoft Copilot usage).
Identification and prioritization of AI use cases with clear ROI, focusing on process-heavy and documentation-heavy workflows.
Definition of at least one “quick win” AI/automation use case (e.g., contracts review, document-heavy workflows) to demonstrate value and ROI.
Architecture recommendation for how potential solutions would be deployed in S.L. Nusbaum’s own Azure environment (LLM selection, hosting pattern, security/governance).
Future-state concept/proposal for a larger, client-facing AI-enabled offering that S.L. Nusbaum can market as part of its services (e.g., to property owners or brokerage clients).

Optionally: Design and build of AI agents/automations (e.g., contract-review assistant, multifamily process automations) either as client-owned solutions in Azure or as access to Coastal’s SaaS products where appropriate.

phases.name: Phase 1 – Discovery & Audit
phases.description: Understand current environment, constraints, and candidate AI use cases across departments; focus on KPIs, OKRs, and ROI.
activities: Audit existing processes, SOPs, data sources, and AI tooling (e.g., current bots, Copilot usage).
Departmental workshops (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily operations, Brokerage) to map major time sinks, manual processes, and documentation-heavy workflows.
Elicit KPIs and OKRs per department to define how AI success will be measured.
Assess existing Azure and Microsoft 365/Copilot setup and primary line-of-business applications (including their native AI features).
dependencies: Access to Mark and selected department heads.
Agreement on governance and security expectations for any discovery and data review.
outputs: Workshop framework and discovery-question pack for internal use by Mark with departments.
Discovery summary listing candidate AI use cases by department, mapped to KPIs/OKRs and estimated impact.
Shortlist of high-ROI “quick win” automations (e.g., contract redlining assistance, document triage, process automation).

phases.name: Phase 2 – Prioritization & POC Design
phases.description: Select and design initial proof(s) of concept to demonstrate ROI and build internal buy-in.
activities: Work with Mark to prioritize use cases based on impact, feasibility, and executive appetite (including marketable/externally-facing opportunities).
Define one or more POCs (e.g., contract-review assistant in Azure; enhanced document-processing workflows) with clear success metrics and ROI model (time saved, error reduction, etc.).
Choose appropriate LLMs and tooling (Azure OpenAI, other models) based on task complexity, cost, and hallucination tolerance.
Design architecture for hosting within S.L. Nusbaum’s Azure tenant (preferred) or as a Coastal-hosted POC if internal infra isn’t ready.
dependencies: Completion of discovery workshops / departmental interviews.
Executive alignment on which business problems to tackle first and budget for consulting hours/POC.
Access to sample data (e.g., contracts, bills, property docs) with appropriate security controls.
outputs: POC design document(s) including architecture, models, tools, data flows, and governance.
ROI and business case materials to support executive committee approval.
Implementation plan and estimated effort/cost for POC(s).

phases.name: Phase 3 – POC Implementation & Evaluation
phases.description: Implement selected POC(s), measure impact, and prepare for potential scale-out.
activities: Build AI agents/automations in S.L. Nusbaum’s Azure environment (or Coastal’s environment for temporary POCs).
Integrate with relevant systems (SharePoint, LOB apps, document repositories, etc.).
User acceptance testing with target users (e.g., admins handling contracts, brokers, accounting staff).
Measure pre- and post-implementation metrics (time spent, error rate, throughput) versus KPIs/OKRs.
Refine prompts, guardrails, and workflows (prompt design, context engineering, hallucination mitigation).
dependencies: Availability of pilot users and sample workloads.
Security and compliance review by Mark’s team where necessary.
outputs: Working POC solution(s) demonstrating measurable ROI.
Before/after metrics demonstrating time savings and process improvements.
Recommendations for productionization and broader rollout (technical and change-management).

phases.name: Phase 4 – Productionization & Marketable Offering
phases.description: Scale successful solutions and, where appropriate, shape them into offerings S.L. Nusbaum can market to its own clients.
activities: Harden and scale successful automations (performance, monitoring, security, support model).
Decide per-solution deployment model: fully tenant-owned in S.L. Nusbaum Azure vs. subscription access to Coastal’s SaaS offerings.
Craft externally-facing value proposition and collateral (how AI-enabled capabilities benefit property owners, tenants, and brokerage clients).
Identify opportunities for verticalized SaaS solutions in real estate/multifamily using components built for S.L. Nusbaum.
dependencies: Successful POC outcomes and executive committee sign-off on further investment.
Alignment on product vs. bespoke-solution strategy.
Ongoing governance model for AI use, including oversight by Mark’s team.
outputs: Production-ready AI workflows and agents in S.L. Nusbaum’s environment.
Externally marketable AI-enabled service descriptions S.L. Nusbaum can present in large deals (e.g., $100–300M transactions).
Roadmap for future enhancements and additional AI initiatives across departments.

timeline.duration: Exploratory pre-engagement period through at least mid-January; subsequent phases TBD based on January discussions and budgeting.

milestones.name: Send workshop framework & discovery questions
milestones.targetDate: Within ~1 week after the November 5 meeting
milestones.details: Dylan to email Mark example workshop structure and department-specific AI discovery questions.

milestones.name: Internal departmental conversations by Mark
milestones.targetDate: Nov–Dec (budget season and departmental head meetings)
milestones.details: Mark to meet with executives, department heads, and brokers to surface AI needs and candidate use cases.

milestones.name: Department head meeting
milestones.targetDate: Later in November (exact date not stated)
milestones.details: Mark expects to get more insight into departmental needs and potential AI use cases.

milestones.name: Brokers’ monthly meeting (with Mark attending)
milestones.details: Mark to join brokers’ meeting in January to gather more detailed and custom AI/automation asks.

milestones.name: Mid-January follow-up meeting
milestones.details: Regroup between Coastal Elements AI and Mark to review discovered opportunities, prioritize, and discuss potential POCs/engagement.

milestones.name: Informal lunch/beers meeting
milestones.details: Dylan and Mark to meet locally for relationship-building and informal discussion of opportunities.

commercialTerms.roiExpectation: Engagements must show clear, quantifiable ROI—primarily via time savings and process improvements in manual/document-heavy workflows—AND, for larger investments, contribute to an externally marketable AI-enabled offering that S.L. Nusbaum can take to its own clients. Mark emphasized that executive committee sign-off will depend on both operational ROI and perceived market value in large ($100–$300M) deals.

nextSteps.action: Schedule mid-January follow-up meeting with Mark to review AI opportunities discovered in internal discussions.
nextSteps.source: "Yeah. You know, maybe mid-January, kind of regroup, and see what conversations we're starting to have, and see if there's an opportunity, and just go from there."

nextSteps.action: Email Mark workshop framework and departmental discovery questions (HR, Finance/Accounting, Multifamily, Brokerage) and examples of how to structure AI workshops.
nextSteps.source: "I'm going to see if I can put over the next, you know, week or so, put together a couple things just to send over, like, hey, you know, this is an example of, like, what a workshop would look like, a framework there, maybe some, you know, questions that might be good from certain departments..."

nextSteps.action: Consider lens of marketable, client-facing AI offerings when drafting workshop materials and use-case suggestions.
nextSteps.source: "I'm also going to be looking at from the lens of not just, hey, am I fixing this one process issue, but is it something that potentially, you know, could be marketable as, hey, we're doing this for, you know, as an organization..."

nextSteps.action: Conduct internal one-on-ones with executives and department heads to identify AI/automation needs, with focus on ROI and marketable offerings.
nextSteps.source: "I think allowing me some time to, you know, really start having these one-on-ones with, you know, the executives and find out what's, you know, where their brains are at."

nextSteps.action: Attend department head meeting later in the month and brokers’ meeting in January to gather detailed AI requests and custom needs.
nextSteps.source: "We have a department head meeting later this month that I think I'm going to get a lot more kind of inside information... And then some of the brokers, they have a monthly meeting that they invited me to in January."

nextSteps.action: Schedule and hold an informal lunch/beers meeting to deepen relationship and discuss AI directions informally before the formal January follow-up.
nextSteps.source: "She was a very Southern woman as well... would love to grab lunch or beers at some point. Practically a neighbor, so I'll see if we can coordinate something."

nextSteps.action: Be prepared to propose example POC scenarios (e.g., contract-review assistant, enhanced document bots) tailored to S.L. Nusbaum’s Azure environment once Mark’s internal discovery is complete.
nextSteps.source: Discussion on building solutions in client’s Azure and on contract/document-heavy workflows.

keyQuotes: "My goal is to really force the vendors into offering this stuff, rather than getting into the trap that the company had been in for the last 15 to 20 years, where there's a bunch of homebrew, one-off, you know, snowflake solutions."

"The amount of time spent by resources doing this versus the amount of time now being spent on that same activity, that shows an ROI there immediately… You don't want to just do AI for the sake of AI."

"For the immediate future, I think allowing me some time to really, you know, start having these conversations… Maybe mid-January, kind of regroup, and see what conversations we're starting to have, and see if there's an opportunity, and just go from there."

"What you're going to start seeing is companies like ours really starting to market what AI solutions they have and how we're offering AI to benefit our customers. And if we're not doing that, then I think that it's going to pass us by."
